headline: Sabah Coral Reef Degradation Reaches Critical 42% Level
context: Marine Research Foundation reported 42% of Sabah coral reefs now classified as degraded or critically damaged based on 2025 surveys.

cards.big: 42% of Sabah coral reefs now classified as degraded or critical
cards.sub: Bleaching dynamite fishing and sediment runoff destroying marine ecosystems

cards.big: Coral cover in Semporna dropped from 65% to 28% between 2010 and 2025
cards.sub: Area home to 560 coral species representing 80% of global reef biodiversity

cards.big: Sabah Parks recorded 85 dynamite fishing incidents in 2025 despite patrols
cards.sub: Each blast destroys average 20 square metres needing 25 years to recover

cards.big: Dive tourism generates RM1.2 billion but declining reefs threaten industry
cards.sub: 65% of 180 dive operators reported significant reef deterioration at key sites

cards.big: Reef decline tests whether marine protection keeps pace with economic pressure on oceans
42% coral degradation — Sabah's reef crisis threatens both marine biodiversity and RM8 billion in tourism.
